2/12
INTRODUCTION

Introduction to Decision Trees

Decision trees are a type of machine learning model used for classification and regression tasks. They learn a hierarchy of if/else questions to make predictions.

1/10
SLIDE 04

Theory of Decision Trees

Decision trees learn a hierarchy of if/else questions, leading to a decision. They can be used for both classification and regression tasks. The algorithm learns the sequence of if/else questions to make predictions. For continuous data, the questions are of the form 'Is feature i larger than value a?'

  • Decision trees learn a hierarchy of if/else questions📄 Page 84
  • Used for classification and regression tasks📄 Page 84
  • Questions for continuous data are of the form 'Is feature i larger than value a?'📄 Page 85
📄 Page 84-85
🔍 Click to enlarge
Figure 2-22. A decision tree to distinguish among several animals — Page 85
2/10
SLIDE 05

Implementation of Decision Trees

A decision tree can be visualized to provide an in-depth view of how the algorithm makes predictions. The tree can be used to distinguish among several animals. For example, a decision tree can be built to classify animals based on features such as the presence of fins.

  • Decision trees can be visualized📄 Page 91
  • Used to distinguish among several animals📄 Page 85
  • Example: classifying animals based on presence of fins📄 Page 84
📄 Page 84-91
🔍 Click to enlarge
Figure 2-23. Two-moons dataset on which the decision tree will be built — Page 86
3/10
SLIDE 06

Parameters and Limitations of Decision Trees

Decision trees tend to overfit the training data. They can be improved by using techniques such as pre-pruning. However, even with pre-pruning, decision trees can still provide poor generalization.

  • Decision trees tend to overfit the training data📄 Page 97
  • Can be improved using pre-pruning📄 Page 97
  • Can still provide poor generalization📄 Page 97
📄 Page 97
🔍 Click to enlarge
Figure 3-2. Effect of scaling training and test data shown on the left together (center) and — Page 151
4/10
SLIDE 07

Ensembles of Decision Trees

Ensembles of decision trees, such as random forests, can improve the performance of decision trees. Random forests work by building multiple decision trees on different subsets of the data and combining their predictions.

  • Ensembles of decision trees can improve performance📄 Page 97
  • Random forests build multiple decision trees on different subsets of the data📄 Page 97
  • Combine predictions of multiple trees📄 Page 98
📄 Page 97-98
🔍 Click to enlarge
Figure 2-32. Comparison of predictions made by a linear model and predictions made — Page 96
5/10
SLIDE 08

Implementation of Ensembles of Decision Trees

Random forests can be used to improve the performance of decision trees. The algorithm works by building multiple decision trees on different subsets of the data and combining their predictions. The result is a more accurate and robust model.

  • Random forests can improve decision tree performance📄 Page 99
  • Build multiple decision trees on different subsets of the data📄 Page 98
  • Combine predictions of multiple trees📄 Page 99
📄 Page 98-99
🔍 Click to enlarge
Figure 2-33. Decision boundaries found by five randomized decision trees and the deci‐ — Page 100
6/10
SLIDE 09

Parameters and Limitations of Ensembles of Decision Trees

The performance of ensembles of decision trees can be improved by tuning parameters such as the number of trees and the maximum depth of the trees. However, increasing the number of trees can also increase the risk of overfitting.

  • Tuning parameters can improve performance📄 Page 102
  • Number of trees and maximum depth are important parameters📄 Page 104
  • Increasing number of trees can increase risk of overfitting📄 Page 104
📄 Page 102-104
🔍 Click to enlarge
Figure 2-1. Trade-off of model complexity against training and test accuracy — Page 43
7/10
SLIDE 10

Feature Importances in Decision Trees

Decision trees and decision tree-based models provide a feature_importances_ attribute that can be used to determine the importance of each feature in the model.

  • Decision trees provide feature_importances_ attribute📄 Page 252
  • Attribute can be used to determine feature importance📄 Page 252
  • Feature importance can be used for feature selection📄 Page 252
📄 Page 252
🔍 Click to enlarge
Figure 2-28. Feature importances computed from a decision tree learned on the Breast — Page 92
8/10
SLIDE 11

Binning, Discretization, Linear Models, and Trees

Binning and discretization are techniques used to transform continuous data into categorical data. Linear models and decision trees can be used to model the transformed data.

  • Binning and discretization transform continuous data📄 Page 234
  • Linear models and decision trees can model transformed data📄 Page 234
  • Decision trees can be used for regression tasks📄 Page 234
📄 Page 234
🔍 Click to enlarge
Figure 4-1. Comparing linear regression and a decision tree on the wave dataset — Page 235
9/10
✦

Summary of Decision Trees

Decision trees are a type of machine learning model used for classification and regression tasks. They can be improved using techniques such as pre-pruning and ensembles of decision trees.

Decision trees
10/10
